Issue: Nominal record declarations and type identity
Acceptance Criteria: record Name { field: type, ... } parses and type-checks as a nominal non-empty record declaration, field names are unique, record identity is nominal rather than structural, and syntax/spec/tests define the Stage-1 non-goals honestly.

Issue: Record construction and canonical runtime carrier
Acceptance Criteria: Record construction Name { field: expr, ... } works end-to-end through SemCode and VM with one deterministic slot-based carrier, each field is provided exactly once, field order is compile-time fixed, and no defaults or hidden heap/object model are introduced.

Issue: Field access and deterministic slot lowering
Acceptance Criteria: value.field resolves only against canonical record declarations, unknown and duplicate field diagnostics are precise, field access lowers through stable deterministic slot operations, and verified-path plus VM tests cover read-only access.

Issue: Record pass, return, and equality-safe comparisons
Acceptance Criteria: Records can bind to locals, pass through function calls, and return as ordinary executable values; record equality is allowed only when every field type already supports stable equality; and docs/tests make the equality-safe subset explicit.

Issue: Stage-1 record contract freeze and scenario validation
Acceptance Criteria: The canonical record spec, diagnostics, and motivating scenarios are updated to match implementation reality, sample workloads compile through the verified path, and D20P-D01 record punning remains explicitly gated until a later destructuring ergonomics decision.
